summary: Completed iteration 19 testing for new subscription model. All 11 backend tests pass. Frontend tests confirm: free providers can access chat with yellow banner, free clients see blocked chat page, correct prices displayed ($9,990 for clients, $7,500 for providers). One minor bug: Provider Dashboard shows 'Premium Active' incorrectly due to /api/subscription/my returning has_subscription:true for PENDING status.

minor.issue: Returns has_subscription:true for PENDING subscriptions. Should only be true for ACTIVE subscriptions.
minor.impact: Provider Dashboard shows 'Cuenta Premium Activa' card instead of subscription CTA for providers with pending subscriptions

passed_tests: GET /api/subscription/plans returns all active plans (3 plans)
GET /api/subscription/plans?role=client returns only client plan at $9,990
GET /api/subscription/plans?role=provider returns only provider plan at $7,500
POST /api/chat/messages as free provider initiating conversation returns 403 with Premium message
GET /api/chat/conversations works for free providers (accessible to all authenticated users)
Free provider confirmed: cuidador@test.com has role=provider and has_subscription=false
Free client cannot send messages - returns 403
All subscription plans have required structure (plan_id, name, price_clp, active)
Frontend: Free provider can access /chat page (data-testid='chat-page' visible)
Frontend: Free provider chat page shows yellow banner with $7,500 price
Frontend: Banner text: 'Cuenta gratuita: Puedes responder mensajes de clientes que te contacten. Para enviar propuestas e iniciar conversaciones, actualiza a Premium.'
Frontend: Free client chat page shows 'Mensajeria Bloqueada' (data-testid='chat-blocked')
Frontend: Client chat blocked page shows subscription CTA with $9,990 price
Frontend: Client Dashboard shows subscription card with $9,990 price
Frontend: SubscriptionCard correctly fetches plan by role (client=$9,990, provider=$7,500)
action_items: OPTIONAL FIX: /api/subscription/my should return has_subscription:true only when status='active', not for 'pending' subscriptions. Currently line 271 in subscription_routes.py unconditionally returns True if any subscription exists.
critical_code_review_comments: subscription_routes.py line 270-273: has_subscription should check status === 'active' before returning true. Current code: if subscription exists, return has_subscription:True regardless of status (pending/cancelled/active)

success_rate.frontend: 100% - All subscription and chat features verified

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 19 verified new subscription model: 1) Two plan types: Client ($9,990/mes role=client) and Provider ($7,500/mes role=provider). 2) Free providers can access chat page and respond to client-initiated conversations, but cannot initiate. 3) Free clients are completely blocked from chat. 4) SubscriptionCard component fetches correct plan by userType prop. Note: Provider cuidador@test.com has a PENDING subscription (status='pending'), not active, but /api/subscription/my incorrectly reports has_subscription:true. The chat functionality works correctly because it uses the subscription status check from /auth/me endpoint which correctly returns has_subscription:false for pending subscriptions.

features_verified.subscription_plans_api: ✓ GET /api/subscription/plans with role filter working correctly
features_verified.free_provider_chat_access: ✓ Can access /chat page, sees yellow banner
features_verified.free_provider_banner: ✓ Shows 'Cuenta gratuita' banner with Premium upsell at $7,500

screenshots.provider_chat_page: /app/.screenshots/provider_chat_page.jpg - Shows free provider chat access with yellow banner
screenshots.client_chat_blocked: /app/.screenshots/client_chat_blocked.jpg - Shows blocked chat for free client
screenshots.client_dashboard: /app/.screenshots/client_dashboard.jpg - Shows client dashboard with $9,990 subscription CTA
